description: Undefined EditorAction variant 'BlurEvent'. The Tier 0 obligation enumerates the EditorAction discriminated union as: 'BlockContentEdited, BlockInserted, BlockRemoved, BlocksMerged, BlockSplit, BlockTypeChanged, BlockMoved, BlockFocused, BlockBlurred, EditorBlurredAllBlocks, DomainSnapshotReceived, BlurEvent, plus the save-action set'. The first eleven names map cleanly to internal-events.ts (capture/internal-events.ts) plus the synthetic DomainSnapshotReceived. 'BlurEvent' has no payload shape, no firing condition, and no producer documented anywhere in either spec file. There is no REQ-EDIT-XXX, no §3.1-§3.11 sub-section, no Glossary entry §6, no Resolved Decision §9, and no proof obligation §4 row that defines BlurEvent. The Tier 0 obligation says the reducer must include this variant — Phase 2 has no way to know what payload to construct or what reducer behaviour to assert. This is a hole in the EditorAction discriminated union contract.

evidence.snippet: **`EditorAction` discriminated union**: `editorReducer.ts` accepts a discriminated union covering all UI-triggered actions including `BlockContentEdited`, `BlockInserted`, `BlockRemoved`, `BlocksMerged`, `BlockSplit`, `BlockTypeChanged`, `BlockMoved`, `BlockFocused`, `BlockBlurred`, `EditorBlurredAllBlocks`, `DomainSnapshotReceived`, `BlurEvent`, plus the save-action set.

suggestedRemediation: Either (a) remove BlurEvent from the enumeration if it is redundant with EditorBlurredAllBlocks (which already covers the all-blocks-blurred case per REQ-EDIT-014), OR (b) define BlurEvent with an explicit payload (e.g., `{ kind: 'BlurEvent'; targetBlockId: BlockId | null; nextFocusTargetWithinEditor: boolean }`) and tie it to a REQ acceptance criterion. Also enumerate the 'save-action set' explicitly (e.g., TriggerIdleSave, TriggerBlurSave, NoteFileSaved, NoteSaveFailed, RetrySave, DiscardCurrentSession, CancelSwitch) so the reducer obligation is closed.
